title: Pakistani Chicken White Karahi

subs.name: 100 ml full-fat milk + 1 tsp lemon juice (left to curdle for 5 minutes)
subs.notes: works as a quick yoghurt substitute

subs.name: 100 ml full-fat coconut cream
subs.notes: dairy-free alternative; slightly different flavour profile

ingredients.name: fresh coriander and ginger julienne for garnish

steps.text: Heat oil and butter together in a karahi over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds and lightly crushed cardamom and sizzle for 20 seconds. Add the grated ginger and garlic and fry for 1 minute until fragrant — they should turn very lightly golden but not brown.

Add the chicken pieces and sear over high heat for 5–6 minutes, turning every minute, until sealed and lightly coloured on all sides. Season generously with salt and add 1 teaspoon of the crushed black pepper.

Beat the curd until smooth. Reduce heat to medium and pour the curd over the chicken, stirring quickly to prevent it from splitting. Add the green chillies. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes until the chicken has absorbed the yoghurt and the sauce has reduced.

Pour in the cream, reduce heat to low-medium, and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring every 3–4 minutes, until the chicken is fully cooked through and the sauce is thick and creamy — it should coat the back of a spoon.
Add the remaining black pepper and garam masala. Taste for salt. Cook for 2 more minutes on high heat to tighten the sauce slightly and give it a light sheen.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and ginger julienne. Serve from the karahi directly with naan — the white karahi's mild flavour is best complemented by a plain bread rather than rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cream → 100 ml full-fat coconut cream (dairy-free alternative; slightly different flavour profile); curd → 100 ml full-fat milk + 1 tsp lemon juice (left to curdle for 5 minutes) (works as a quick yoghurt substitute); butter → 2 tbsp ghee (richer, more aromatic)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This dish has a very close Indian cousin in malai chicken tikka masala. Cook it in a kadai on medium-low heat — cream-based sauces must not boil hard or they split. Thick full-fat Amul or Milky Mist cream works best. The dish pairs beautifully with Indian tandoori naan or a soft laccha paratha from any Indian bakery.
